Earth is the third planet from the Sun in the solar system and the only planet known to harbor life.
Learning that the speed of light—the fastest known speed in the universe—is approximately 300,000 kilometers per second in a vacuum left me both awestruck and humbled by the vastness of the universe.

To analyze the artistic conception of an ancient poem, one must first master the three fundamental expressive techniques—fu (narrative description), bi (analogy), and xing (evocative association)—and then interpret the poem in light of the poet's life experiences.

The Pythagorean theorem states that in a right triangle, the sum of the squares of the two legs equals the square of the hypotenuse. This theorem was known as the 'Shang Gao Theorem' in ancient China.

The most common methods for solving quadratic equations are completing the square, using the quadratic formula, and factoring. I usually begin by using the discriminant to determine the nature of the roots and then select an appropriate method.

Calibrating satellite remote sensing: acquiring on-board blackbody data → establishing a radiative transfer model → fitting the nonlinear response → generating a lookup table (LUT) → injecting it into the ground system. When the sea surface temperature retrieval error is ≤0.3 K, I affirm: the dignity of space-based observation lies in every bit of data bearing an exact promise to Earth.

Running the Community Earth System Model (CESM): coupling atmospheric, oceanic, land surface, and sea ice components → millennium-long spin-up → industrial revolution forcing → RCP scenario simulations → statistical analysis of extreme events; once the increase in heatwave frequency matches observations within a 5% margin of error, I affirm: planetary-scale modeling is humanity’s most solemn sandbox for exploring future possibilities.

Deciphering chromatin accessibility using single-cell ATAC-seq: nuclear isolation → Tn5 transposition → library construction → sequencing → peak calling → analysis of differentially accessible regions. When I discovered newly identified enhancers regulating neurodevelopmental genes, I realized that the epigenetic workflow enables silent DNA to speak a decipherable molecular language.

Building microbial factories using synthetic biology: designing metabolic pathways → gene assembly → host transformation → fermentation optimization → product extraction → purity testing. When the artemisinic acid titer exceeds 12 g/L, I realize that programming life compresses hundreds of millions of years of biochemistry into tunable genetic circuits.

Debugging invasive brain-computer interface implants: preoperative fMRI localization → intraoperative microelectrode recording → signal decoding → closed-loop stimulation → long-term immune response monitoring. When a paralyzed patient mentally controls a robotic arm to drink water, I understand that the neural interface process translates the electrical currents of thought into a gentle handshake with the physical world.

Activating senescence-related genes using CRISPRa: design dCas9-VP64 → AAV delivery → single-cell RNA-seq → trajectory analysis → phenotypic validation. When synaptic density in the mouse hippocampus increased by 31%, I understood the epigenetic editing process as a careful attempt to reverse the flow of time’s river along a specific stretch.

Developing a lunar south pole water-ice detection radar: ultra-wideband antenna array → pulse compression → ground-penetrating capability → dielectric inversion → 3D imaging → signal-to-noise ratio enhancement. Once a water-rich layer over 10 meters thick is identified within a permanently shadowed region, I confirm that the extraterrestrial exploration process translates electromagnetic wave reflections into a site-selection blueprint for humanity’s future home.

Debugging the fusion device tokamak: evacuate to vacuum of 10⁻⁸ Pa → inject deuterium and tritium → heat with neutral beams → confine magnetically → monitor neutron flux → analyze first-wall thermal load; when the Q value exceeds 1.5, I realize: the process of creating an artificial sun transforms Einstein’s mass-energy equivalence equation into the ultimate battery sustaining human civilization.

Observing protein conformational changes using single-molecule fluorescence resonance energy transfer (smFRET): immobilizing single molecules → dual-color excitation → nanosecond-scale photon counting → calculation of the Förster distance → modeling of dynamic distributions. Upon discovering folding intermediates of Alzheimer’s disease–related proteins, I realized: the biological process is a high-definition documentary translating nanosecond-scale molecular motions into disease mechanisms.

Developing the Mars In-Situ Resource Utilization (ISRU) oxygen generator MOXIE: CO₂ collection → compression → electrochemical decomposition → O₂ purification → pressure regulation → compatibility testing with the Martian atmosphere. When MOXIE first produced 5.37 grams of oxygen, I confirmed: the interplanetary survival process translates the thin, red planet’s breath into the first breath of oxygen for human footsteps.

Performing numerical simulations of quantum gravity theory: discrete spacetime lattice → loop quantum geometry → spin foam evolution → correlation function calculation → renormalization group flow analysis; when the low-energy limit reproduces the general relativity term, I understand that the fundamental physical process involves projecting human mathematical intuition onto the subtlest threads and weaves of spacetime.

Storing data using synthetic DNA: binary encoding → DNA sequence design → oligonucleotide synthesis → chip integration → PCR amplification → nanopore sequencing → error correction. When the end-to-end read/write fidelity for 1 MB of data reaches 99.99%, I confirm: the information immortality process embeds humanity’s bits onto life’s most ancient molecular carrier.
The cell is the basic structural and functional unit of living organisms, composed of components such as the cell membrane, cytoplasm, and nucleus. Plant cells also have a cell wall.

When observing plant cells, iodine solution is typically used to stain the specimen so that the cell nucleus becomes more visible. When preparing a temporary slide, care must be taken to avoid introducing air bubbles.
Dream of the Red Chamber was originally titled The Story of the Stone and is the foremost of China's Four Great Classical Novels, acclaimed as an encyclopedia of Chinese feudal society.

When analyzing the personalities of fictional characters, one can begin from four dimensions—physical appearance, speech, actions, and psychological descriptions—while also incorporating typical plot events for analysis.
The periodic table is a list of chemical elements arranged in ascending order of atomic number, revealing the intrinsic relationships among elements and their periodic patterns.

The Kaiyuan Golden Age of the Tang Dynasty was the zenith of Emperor Xuanzong's reign, marked by remarkable prosperity in both the economy and culture, with Chang'an emerging as an international metropolis.

Studying historical events requires employing multiple methodologies—including documentary analysis, comparative research, and quantitative historiography—to achieve objectivity and comprehensiveness.
English has 26 letters, of which the vowels—A, E, I, O, and U—are central to pronouncing English words.
The most effective way to improve spoken English is through immersive learning, such as watching English-language movies and shadowing them, or practicing with a language partner.
Newton's first law states that an object remains at rest or in uniform linear motion unless acted upon by an external force, illustrating the object's inertia.

To solve a mechanics problem, one must first perform a force analysis on the object under study and draw a clear free-body diagram, then establish a system of equations using an appropriate coordinate system based on the object's state of motion.
Supply and demand are the most fundamental concepts in economics; their interaction jointly determines the market price and output of goods.
Analyzing a country's macroeconomic health typically involves a comprehensive assessment of three core indicators: GDP growth rate, inflation rate, and unemployment rate.
Among the eight planets in the solar system, Jupiter has the largest volume and mass, earning it the title of 'King of the Planets.'

To observe planets and deep-sky objects, it is best to use a large-aperture astronomical telescope and select an observation site in the suburbs with minimal light pollution and excellent atmospheric seeing.
The Five Great Mountains refer to China's five most famous mountains: Mount Tai (East Peak), Mount Hua (West Peak), Mount Heng (South Peak), Mount Heng (North Peak), and Mount Song (Central Peak).

Monet was a leading figure and one of the founders of Impressionism; his 'Water Lilies' series focuses on the subtle variations in light, shadow, and color of the same scene under different lighting conditions.

I gradually came to understand that Impressionist painters were not seeking to reproduce objects with precision, but rather to capture and convey the visual sensations and light-and-shadow impressions of a given moment.
The Analects is one of the most important Confucian classics, recording the words, deeds, and ideas of Confucius and his disciples in an aphoristic style.

The pyramids were tombs built for ancient Egyptian pharaohs; the largest, the Great Pyramid of Khufu, stands approximately 146 meters tall and is constructed from about 2.3 million massive stone blocks, making it one of the wonders of the world.

While watching a historical exploration documentary, I learned that the massive stones of the pyramids fit together so precisely that not even a thin blade can be inserted between them—an astonishing feat.

Constructing massive structures such as the pyramids required extremely precise planning and design, rigorous organizational management, and highly efficient coordination of vast human and material resources.

The international unit of electric current is the ampere, commonly abbreviated as 'amp' and symbolized as 'A,' in honor of the French physicist André-Marie Ampère for his outstanding contributions to the field of electromagnetism.
Vitamin C is a water-soluble vitamin essential to the human body and is widely found in fresh fruits and vegetables; prolonged deficiency can lead to scurvy.
To maintain good health, it is necessary to consume a balanced diet every day, including appropriate amounts of protein, healthy fats, vitamins, and minerals.
I heard that kiwifruit contains several times more vitamin C than oranges, so I’ve been eating one every day recently—and I feel my skin condition and energy levels have improved.

Peking Opera facial makeup features rich colors: red generally symbolizes loyalty, bravery, and chivalry, as exemplified by Guan Yu; white represents treachery and deceit, as embodied by Cao Cao; and black signifies integrity and uprightness, as personified by Bao Zheng.

I have gradually come to understand that the charm of Beijing Opera lies in its integration of singing, spoken dialogue, acting, and martial arts—using highly stylized yet aesthetically rich performances to portray the myriad facets of human life.
A modern computer's hardware system primarily consists of core components such as the central processing unit (CPU), memory, hard disk drive, motherboard, power supply, and graphics card.
Earth's atmosphere is vertically stratified from bottom to top as the troposphere, stratosphere, mesosphere, thermosphere (also known as the warm layer), and exosphere.

Accurate weather forecasting requires a vast global network of meteorological stations to collect ground- and upper-air observational data, followed by complex numerical simulations performed on supercomputers.
Water boils at 100 degrees Celsius and freezes at 0 degrees Celsius under standard atmospheric pressure.
Yesterday, in physics class, I learned that the speed of light in a vacuum is approximately 3×10^8 meters per second; after writing it down, I found the concept fascinating.
Mammals are characterized by viviparity, lactation, and having hair or fur covering their bodies—key features that distinguish them from other vertebrates.

Last week, I went to the library to research some materials and learned that Earth takes approximately 365.24 days to complete one orbit around the Sun—that is, one tropical year. While organizing my notes, I marveled at how extraordinary the universe’s laws truly are.

Chinese characters are primarily created using six methods: pictographs, simple ideograms, compound ideograms, phono-semantic compounds, derivative cognates, and phonetic loan characters; among these, phono-semantic compounds constitute the largest proportion.

I memorized yesterday while learning vocabulary that the general rule for forming plurals of nouns in English is to add -s to the end of the word, and to add -es to words ending in s, x, ch, or sh. After memorizing 50 words, I felt a great sense of accomplishment.
When cooking rice, use a rice-to-water ratio of 1:1.2, a proportion derived from rice’s water absorption rate, yielding rice with an ideal texture—neither too soft nor too hard.
The sum of the interior angles of a triangle is always 180 degrees, regardless of whether it is an acute, right, or obtuse triangle.

Folding an emergency blanket: fold in half along the centerline → fold again into a strip → roll tightly in a spiral → insert into the fastener strap. After the first unfolding failed, I consulted reference materials and learned that its principle relies on a vacuum-deposited aluminum layer reflecting the human body's infrared radiation—the entire process is grounded in basic thermodynamic principles.

Verifying the square root using the Feynman algorithm: guess x → compute y = (x + n/x)/2 → stop if |y - x| < ε; otherwise, set x = y and continue. Convergence is slow on the third iteration; upon revisiting the derivation, it becomes clear that the initial guess affects the O(log log n) complexity.

Analyzing user needs using the KANO model: basic (the charging port does not break), performance (30-minute fast charging to 50%), and delighter (magnetic auto-alignment). The second version of the survey questionnaire omitted the 'seamless interaction' dimension; only upon revisiting users did we learn that 'not having to think' represents the highest-level user experience.

Write advertising copy using the AIDA model: Attention (lightning screen-split animation) → Interest ('You waste 23 minutes daily searching for files') → Desire (AI-powered automatic file archiving + voice-enabled instant search) → Action (Scan the QR code to claim a 7-day VIP trial). The second version had a low click-through rate; A/B testing revealed that 'Interest' metrics must be tied to users' device IDs to be reliable.

Waxing solid wood flooring: sweep and remove dust → wipe clean with a slightly damp mop → apply a thin, even layer of wax → buff with a dry cloth → avoid walking on the floor for 24 hours. On the fourth application, white marks appeared; consulting a materials science report revealed that ‘buffing’ is essentially a phase transition process wherein wax molecules undergo directional realignment under friction-induced heat.

Apply the MECE principle to deconstruct 'enhancing team well-being': (1) Material (compensation/benefits), (2) Relationships (trust/collaboration), (3) Growth (learning/promotion), (4) Meaning (mission/values), and (5) Health (sleep/exercise). During the second consensus meeting, it becomes clear that 'meaning' should be measured through employees’ self-initiated stories rather than KPIs.

Assessing internal controls using the COSO framework: Control Environment → Risk Assessment → Control Activities → Information and Communication → Monitoring Activities. During the second audit round, a deficiency in 'Information and Communication' was identified: keyword alerts were not configured in the DingTalk group. Blind spots in digital processes often exist within the most familiar tools.

Solving the 'hard-to-open delivery box' problem using TRIZ theory: the Trimming Principle (eliminating tape) → Dynamicity (easy-tear perforated lines) → Intermediary (integrated box-opening blade) → Increasing Asymmetry (reinforced tear-off on one side). The third prototype was rejected; user testing revealed that 'easy-tear' functionality must balance child safety and elderly users’ grip strength—technical solutions are always constrained by ergonomic boundaries.

Update the product failure rate using Bayes' theorem: the prior is set to the historical mean of 2.3%; in a new batch, 3 defective units are found among 500 sampled units; the posterior probability becomes 1.8%. After this second update, the team abandons its obsession with 'zero defects' and shifts toward continuous improvement that accepts controllable risk.

Risk analysis of surgical robots using FMEA: Severity (S) → Occurrence (O) → Detection (D) → RPN = S × O × D. During the initial assessment, “intraoperative network latency” was overlooked; however, simulation exercises revealed that a 500-ms delay caused slight tremors in the instruments—thus, the process risk map must include all non-technical variables.

Clarify project roles using the RACI matrix: Responsible (developers write code) → Accountable (CTO signs off) → Consulted (legal reviews terms) → Informed (all-staff email). During the second round of execution, it was discovered that no response deadline had been set for the 'Consulted' role, causing contract delays—process authority begins with defining time granularity.

Analyze the production line yield decline using a Fishbone diagram: People (inadequate training) → Machines (excessive vibration of the pick-and-place machine) → Materials (abnormal solder paste batch) → Methods (reflow profile deviation) → Environment (humidity > 70%) → Measurement (AOI parameters not updated). The root cause was identified as 'Environment' in the third round; however, the humidifier’s fault code was misread as 'normal'—a fatal flaw in the data workflow stemming from missing metadata.

Prioritize requirements using the MoSCoW method: Must have (login security) → Should have (multilingual support) → Could have (dark mode) → Won’t have (blockchain-based evidence preservation). During the second review round, it was discovered that the encryption strength level for ‘Must’ had not been defined; consequently, compliance with the Chinese national cryptography standard SM4 was added—priority setting is not merely sequencing, but rather delineating responsibility boundaries.

Last week, I attended a popular science lecture and learned that the human body has 206 bones, divided into three parts: the skull, the trunk bones, and the limb bones. After the lecture, I gained new insights into human anatomy.
The eight planets of the solar system, ordered from closest to farthest from the Sun, are: Mercury, Venus, Earth, Mars, Jupiter, Saturn, Uranus, and Neptune.

While learning PowerPoint creation, I mastered the use of slide masters, which serve to unify the layout and style of a presentation. I used the slide master to design my company's reporting PowerPoint, resulting in a more standardized overall appearance.

A year has 12 months: January, March, May, July, August, October, and December each have 31 days; April, June, September, and November each have 30 days; and February has 28 days in a common year and 29 days in a leap year.
I used the Pythagorean theorem yesterday while doing math problems—the formula is a² + b² = c², applicable to right triangles—and after solving 10 problems, I became more familiar with this theorem.
The formula for photosynthesis is carbon dioxide + water → organic compounds (with energy stored) + oxygen; it occurs in chloroplasts and requires light.

Last week, during history class, I learned that Emperor Qin Shi Huang unified the six warring states in 221 BCE and standardized writing, currency, and weights and measures; while reviewing my notes, I organized the entire chronology of the Qin Dynasty.

The units of the Chinese yuan are yuan, jiao, and fen: 1 yuan = 10 jiao, and 1 jiao = 10 fen. Currently circulating banknotes include denominations of 100 yuan, 50 yuan, 20 yuan, 10 yuan, 5 yuan, and 1 yuan.

I used this yesterday while grocery shopping—the common methods for storing vegetables: wrap leafy greens in plastic wrap and refrigerate them; store potatoes in a cool, dry place. Vegetables stored this way last longer—very practical!

The first 20 elements in the periodic table of chemical elements are: hydrogen, helium, lithium, beryllium, boron, carbon, nitrogen, oxygen, fluorine, neon, sodium, magnesium, aluminum, silicon, phosphorus, sulfur, chlorine, argon, potassium, and calcium.
The largest country in the world by area is Russia, followed by Canada, China, the United States, and Brazil.

While memorizing English grammar yesterday, I learned that the structure of negative sentences in the simple present tense is: subject + don't/doesn't + base form of the verb. After completing 20 practice questions, my accuracy rate reached 90%.

Basic plant care knowledge: Succulents are drought-tolerant and require watering only once a week; pothos prefers shade and needs consistently moist soil. The succulents and pothos I grow are both thriving.
The three elements of force are magnitude, direction, and point of application; the unit of force is the newton, symbolized as N.

Precautions for heating food in a microwave oven: Do not heat food in metal containers; pierce holes in sealed food before heating; adjust heating time according to the amount of food. I find it very convenient to heat milk in the microwave oven.

Tang dynasty poetry is primarily categorized into five-character quatrains, seven-character quatrains, five-character regulated verse, and seven-character regulated verse; Du Fu’s ‘Ascending the Heights’ is a representative example of seven-character regulated verse.

Yesterday, while organizing the refrigerator, I remembered that the refrigerator compartment should be set to 0–4°C and the freezer compartment to around -18°C; after adjusting the temperatures, food stays fresher, giving me a real sense of accomplishment.
Common insulators include rubber, glass, ceramics, and plastics, while conductors include metals, the human body, the earth, and aqueous solutions of acids, bases, and salts.
Principles for making fruit salad: Choose fruits with a moderate texture—such as strawberries, bananas, and apples—and add yogurt for flavor. My family loves the fruit salad I make.
Leap year determination method: A year is a leap year if it is divisible by 4 but not by 100, or if it is divisible by 400.
China's Four Great Inventions are papermaking, printing, gunpowder, and the compass—the latter of which was first applied to navigation.

The correct way to charge your smartphone: Charge it when the battery level drops below 20%, unplug it promptly once fully charged, and avoid overcharging—this helps extend battery life. My phone’s battery remains in excellent condition even after two years of use.

Common rhetorical devices include metaphor, personification, parallelism, hyperbole, rhetorical question, and counter-question. Metaphors are further categorized into explicit metaphor, implicit metaphor, and metonymic metaphor.

Yesterday, while memorizing geography concepts, I learned that the equator is the 0° latitude line, dividing the Northern and Southern Hemispheres, and the Prime Meridian is the 0° longitude line, dividing east and west longitudes; after writing them down silently three times, I remembered them completely.
The basic building blocks of proteins are amino acids; the human body requires eight essential amino acids, which must be obtained from food.

Last week, I attended a finance lecture and learned the compound interest formula: F = P(1 + i)^n. After understanding it, I calculated the returns on a deposit and found that long-term deposits are more profitable.

The correct steps for washing dishes are: first scrape off food residue, then wash with dishwashing detergent, rinse thoroughly with clean water, dry, and store—this ensures cleaner and more hygienic dishes.
Jupiter is the largest planet in the solar system, while Saturn has prominent rings—a key distinguishing feature between the two.
Laundry tips: Turn colored clothes inside out before washing, avoid direct sunlight when drying, and wash wool garments by hand in cold water to prevent fading and deformation.

Tone pattern rules for classical Chinese poetry: level tones include the first and second tones, while oblique tones include the third, fourth, and entering tones—this is the core rule of regulated verse.
The formula for the circumference of a circle is C = 2πr, and the formula for its area is S = πr², where π is approximately 3.1415926.
China's Five Great Mountains are Mount Tai (East Mountain), Mount Hua (West Mountain), Mount Heng (South Mountain), Mount Heng (North Mountain), and Mount Song (Central Mountain).

Bicycle safety rules: Ride on the right side of the road, signal before turning, and never ride against traffic. Following these rules makes cycling safer—I commute to work by bike every day without any issues.
Common punctuation marks include the period, comma, question mark, exclamation point, enumeration comma, semicolon, and colon, each governed by distinct usage rules.

Yesterday, while memorizing biology concepts, I remembered that the cell is the basic structural and functional unit of living organisms, and the differences between animal and plant cells lie in the presence or absence of a cell wall and chloroplasts; after writing them from memory, all answers were correct.
Sound propagation requires a medium; sound cannot travel through a vacuum, and the speed of sound in air is approximately 340 meters per second.
The world's four oceans, ranked by area from largest to smallest, are the Pacific Ocean, the Atlantic Ocean, the Indian Ocean, and the Arctic Ocean.

Yesterday, while learning English phonetic symbols, I memorized that the 48 International Phonetic Alphabet (IPA) symbols consist of 20 vowels and 28 consonants. Mastering these symbols leads to more accurate word pronunciation and faster vocabulary acquisition.

The essence of a chemical change is the formation of new substances, whereas no new substances are formed during a physical change—for example, rusting of iron is a chemical change, while freezing of water is a physical change.

Photography composition techniques: The rule of thirds divides the frame into a 3x3 grid; placing the main subject at one of the four intersection points results in more aesthetically pleasing photos, which often receive many likes on social media.

Traditional Chinese festivals include the Spring Festival, Lantern Festival, Qingming Festival, Dragon Boat Festival, Qixi Festival, Mid-Autumn Festival, and Double Ninth Festival, each with its own distinct customs.

Basic knowledge of keeping pet cats: Cats need regular deworming, daily cleaning of the litter box, and a diet primarily consisting of cat food, with occasional cat treats. My cat is very healthy and lively.
The unit of voltage is the volt (V), the unit of current is the ampere (A), and the unit of resistance is the ohm (Ω). Ohm’s law is expressed by the formula I = U/R.

Last week, I visited an art museum and learned that oil paint is made by mixing pigment powder with turpentine; different brushstrokes can convey different textures, and I gained a great deal from appreciating masterpieces.
Common meteorological disasters include typhoons, heavy rain, flooding, droughts, and cold waves, each with its own distinct warning signals.

The Four Books consist of The Great Learning, The Doctrine of the Mean, The Analects, and The Mencius; the Five Classics comprise The Book of Songs, The Book of Documents, The Book of Rites, The Book of Changes, and The Spring and Autumn Annals.
The six essential nutrients required by the human body are carbohydrates, proteins, fats, vitamins, minerals, and water—none of which can be omitted.

While memorizing historical facts yesterday, I remembered that the Opium War broke out in 1840 and marked the beginning of modern Chinese history; after organizing a timeline, the historical narrative became much clearer.

Common crystals include table salt, sugar, and ice, while non-crystalline substances include glass, rosin, and asphalt. Crystals have a definite melting point, whereas non-crystalline substances do not.

Last week, I visited the botanical garden and learned that the lotus is an aquatic plant whose rhizomes are called lotus roots and whose seeds are called lotus seeds. It blooms in summer and has high ornamental and culinary value.
China's provincial-level administrative divisions include 23 provinces, 5 autonomous regions, 4 municipalities directly under the central government, and 2 special administrative regions.

While practicing English speaking yesterday, I remembered that common everyday greetings include expressions such as 'How are you?' and 'Nice to meet you!'—and I can use them fluently during conversations with my native English-speaking tutor, giving me great confidence.
Inertia is the property of an object to maintain its original state of motion; Newton's first law is also known as the law of inertia, and all objects possess inertia.

Basic essay structure: introduce the topic in the opening paragraph, elaborate in detail in the body, and elevate the theme in the conclusion. Essays written following this structure are praised by teachers for their clear logic and well-defined themes.
The world's three major religions are Christianity, Islam, and Buddhism, each with distinct beliefs and cultural traditions.
The common reactivity series of metals is: potassium, calcium, sodium, magnesium, aluminum, zinc, iron, tin, lead, hydrogen, copper, mercury, silver, platinum, gold.

Last week, I attended a literature lecture and learned that the three essential elements of a novel are characters, plot, and setting. The plot comprises the beginning, development, climax, and conclusion. We analyzed the plot of 'Kong Yiji,' which deepened my understanding.
The basic functions of money are as a unit of account, medium of exchange, store of value, means of payment, and world currency; among these, the unit of account is the most fundamental function.

Tips for removing clothing stains: apply dishwashing liquid to oil stains, soak juice stains in saltwater, wipe ink stains with alcohol—different stains require different methods, and the clothes will be cleaned thoroughly.
China's Four Great Classical Novels are 'Dream of the Red Chamber,' 'Romance of the Three Kingdoms,' 'Water Margin,' and 'Journey to the West,' each possessing unique literary value.

Yesterday, during a small science experiment, I verified that the magnitude of buoyant force equals the weight of the displaced liquid (Archimedes’ principle); after the experiment succeeded, my understanding of this principle became much clearer.

Newton's first law states that an object not subject to external forces remains at rest or in uniform linear motion; upon reflection, I realized it reveals the deeper premise of inertial reference frames.
The fact that human DNA contains approximately 3 billion base pairs and encodes roughly 20,000 genes leaves me awestruck by the precision and fragility of life's information compression.

Pi (π) is the ratio of a circle's circumference to its diameter—a transcendental number that is irrational and non-repeating. From this, I perceive the eternal certainty embedded within mathematical constants.

The United Nations was founded on October 24, 1945, with its headquarters in New York; its Charter establishes the principle of sovereign equality—a point that prompts me to reflect on the tension between ideals and reality in the international order.

'Subject-Verb-Object' is the fundamental syntactic structure of Chinese, yet subjects are frequently omitted in dialects and spoken language—a realization that highlighted to me how grammatical rules possess both universality and contextual flexibility.

Penicillin was accidentally discovered by Fleming in 1928 and later purified and mass-produced by Chain and Florey; this episode in scientific history taught me that accidental discoveries must undergo rigorous verification before they can become reliable knowledge.

Mammals share several key characteristics, including warm-bloodedness, live birth, and lactation; understanding this has helped me grasp the logical foundation of biological classification more clearly.
Pi (π) is an irrational number, approximately equal to 3.14159; its infinite, non-repeating nature reveals to me the coexistence of certainty and inexhaustibility in mathematics.

The DNA double helix structure was proposed by Watson and Crick in 1953; this discovery not only defined the starting point of modern molecular biology but also led me to reflect on how scientific breakthroughs often arise from interdisciplinary insights.

The acceleration due to gravity at Earth’s surface is approximately 9.8 m/s²; consequently, the velocity of a freely falling object increases linearly with time—a realization that prompted me to reconsider the tangible manifestation of ‘uniform change’ in the real world.
In English, 'serendipity' refers to the ability to make valuable discoveries by chance; the very existence of this word attests to how language encapsulates human cognitive experience.
The periodic table, arranged by atomic number, reveals the periodic patterns in elemental properties—a sense of order that fills me with profound awe at nature's intelligibility.

Conditioned reflexes were established by Pavlov through his experiments with dogs, demonstrating that learning can occur without conscious involvement—this prompted me to reflect on the neural basis of habit formation.

The sum of the interior angles of a triangle is always 180°, a fundamental theorem of Euclidean geometry that prompts me to reflect on how an axiomatic system supports the entire edifice of knowledge.
Viruses lack independent metabolic capabilities and must replicate inside living cells; this definition has led me to reconsider the scientific rigor involved in defining the boundaries of 'life.'

In Chinese, the term 'jiāng' specifically refers to major rivers (e.g., the Yangtze River), whereas 'hé' is predominantly used for northern rivers (e.g., the Yellow River); this geographical naming distinction reflects the deep-seated linkage between language and river-basin civilizations.

Penicillin was accidentally discovered by Fleming in 1928; its antibacterial mechanism lies in inhibiting bacterial cell wall synthesis—a serendipitous event in the history of science underpinned by solid preparatory knowledge.

Negative numbers were already applied in the Chinese mathematical text 'The Nine Chapters on the Mathematical Art' from the 2nd century BCE, where they were used to represent debts—demonstrating that abstract mathematical concepts often originate from real societal needs.

The speed of light in vacuum is 299,792,458 m/s and represents the universal speed limit for information transfer; this limit revealed to me the fundamental constraints that physical laws impose on causal structure.
'Culture' in anthropology refers to a shared system of symbols, values, and practices; after grasping this definition, I began to notice the implicit norms underlying everyday behaviors.

The theory of plate tectonics explains the origins of earthquakes, volcanoes, and mountain ranges; its core principle is that mantle convection drives the movement of the lithosphere—a vast-scale, slow force that shapes our ever-changing world beneath our feet.

Recursion in computer science refers to a function calling itself; the Fibonacci sequence is a classic example. This self-referential structure reminds me of the reflexivity of language and consciousness.

Mendel discovered the laws of segregation and independent assortment of hereditary factors (later termed genes) through his pea plant experiments, introducing predictability into biology for the first time using quantitative methods.

Carbon dioxide is a greenhouse gas, and its atmospheric concentration has risen from 280 ppm before the Industrial Revolution to over 420 ppm today—a figure that weighs heavily on me as a profound recognition of intergenerational responsibility.

'Homophones' refer to Chinese characters that share the same pronunciation but have different meanings (e.g., 'huā' and 'huá'), a phenomenon of dissociation among form, sound, and meaning that highlights the unique evolutionary path of Chinese characters as logographic-syllabic writing.

Newton's first law states that an object remains at rest or in uniform linear motion when no external force acts upon it—a counterintuitive conclusion that overturned the Aristotelian tradition and taught me to question empirical appearances.

Monotremes—mammals such as the platypus—lay eggs yet secrete milk; this seemingly 'contradictory' trait precisely illustrates that evolution proceeds through gradual modification rather than perfect design.

The HTTP protocol specifies how clients and servers exchange hypertext information, and its status codes (e.g., 404) have become part of the universal digital lexicon—technical standards subtly reshaping everyday language.

In Riemannian geometry, parallel lines can intersect, providing the mathematical framework for general relativity and illustrating how abstract mathematics is often validated before its physical reality.

'Threshold effect' refers to the phenomenon where a stimulus must reach a certain intensity to trigger a response; the action potential of a neuron is a prime example—qualitative change following gradual, incremental buildup, much like a critical leap in personal growth.

Radiocarbon dating determines the age of organic materials based on carbon-14’s half-life of 5,730 years; the statistical nature of radioactive decay allows the 'past' to be measured probabilistically.

The critical period hypothesis of language acquisition posits that children more easily master native-language pronunciation and grammar before puberty, reminding us that educational interventions must respect the biologically determined time window.

Topology studies properties of geometric figures that remain unchanged under continuous deformations—for instance, a coffee cup and a doughnut are homeomorphic. This abstract invariance has surprisingly become the key to understanding the robustness of complex systems.

'Ecosystem services' refer to the benefits that nature provides to humans—such as provisioning (e.g., food), regulating (e.g., climate regulation), and cultural (e.g., aesthetic) services. This concept has led me to reinterpret forests not merely as 'resources' but as 'life-support networks.'

Bayes' theorem updates posterior judgments using prior probabilities; its formula, P(H|E) = P(E|H)P(H)/P(E), reveals that rationality is not about clinging to beliefs but rather about dynamically calibrating them in light of evidence.

Oracle bone script is the earliest known mature Chinese writing system, inscribed on turtle shells and animal bones to record divination practices of the Shang Dynasty; from its inception, this script served three interrelated functions: asserting authority, preserving memory, and expressing faith.

Quantum superposition refers to a particle's ability to exist in multiple states simultaneously until observation causes wavefunction collapse—a counterintuitive fact that continually challenges my naive understanding of 'reality'.

'Inflation' refers to a sustained decline in the purchasing power of money; the Consumer Price Index (CPI) is a commonly used measure. Historical data shows that hyperinflation is often accompanied by the collapse of social trust, revealing how knowledge illuminates the institutional foundations.
CRISPR-Cas9 is a gene-editing tool derived from bacterial immune mechanisms—a natural defense strategy that, once deciphered by humans, has become a scalpel for rewriting the code of life.

'The halo effect' is a psychological concept referring to the phenomenon where an outstanding impression of one trait of an individual influences the overall evaluation. Becoming aware of its existence was my first conscious step toward combating bias.

Tides are caused by the gravitational differential between the Moon and the Sun, with the maximum tidal range reaching up to 15 meters (e.g., the Bay of Fundy); the celestial bodies’ gravitational forces gently yet inexorably reshape coastlines twice daily.

'Meme' originally referred to the basic unit of cultural information replication; when Dawkins introduced this concept, he did not anticipate that it would evolve into a synonym for internet image dissemination—theoretical vitality often emerges through cross-disciplinary application.

Graphene is a two-dimensional crystal composed of a single layer of carbon atoms, known for its exceptional strength and excellent electrical conductivity. Its discovery earned the 2010 Nobel Prize, demonstrating how fundamental research—though seemingly impractical—ultimately shapes the future.

Hubble's law indicates that a galaxy's recession velocity is proportional to its distance, demonstrating that the universe is expanding—a key observational finding that transformed the question of 'origins' from a theological proposition into a testable scientific problem.

'Context' in linguistics refers to the physical, social, and psychological environment in which discourse occurs; the same utterance—such as 'It's cold'—carries vastly different meanings in an air-conditioned room versus at a breakup scene, reminding us that meaning always arises from relationships.

Insulin, secreted by pancreatic beta cells, regulates glucose uptake into cells; its deficiency leads to diabetes—a microscopic molecular event that can determine macroscopic quality of life, filling me with awe at the body's exquisitely delicate balance.

'Emergence' refers to the phenomenon in complex systems where higher-level novel properties arise from interactions among lower-level components (e.g., neurons → consciousness); it rejects reductionism while leaving room for scientific humility in understanding consciousness.

The Code of Hammurabi is inscribed on a basalt stele and is renowned for its 'an eye for an eye' principle. It is one of the earliest known written legal codes; the physical nature of the legal text allows its authority to remain tangible across four millennia.

'Dark matter' does not emit or absorb light, yet its existence is confirmed through gravitational effects; it accounts for 27% of the universe's total mass-energy. Its 'invisibility' is precisely science's most powerful declaration: reality does not equal visibility.

'Coevolution' refers to the mutual evolutionary process in which two or more species exert selective pressures on each other, such as pollinating insects and flowering plants—life has never been a solo performance, but rather an evolutionary chronicle of symbiotic dance spanning hundreds of millions of years.

'Thesaurus' is a semantic tool in information science used to standardize term relationships, constructing conceptual networks with symbols such as 'USE,' 'UF,' and 'BT'; knowledge organization itself constitutes a silent form of mental modeling.

The second law of thermodynamics states that the entropy of an isolated system never decreases; the universe will ultimately tend toward heat death—a stark conclusion that, paradoxically, deepens my appreciation for the present moment of order.

'The Other' in philosophy refers to a heterogeneous entity constructed within the subject's cognition; after understanding this concept, I become more vigilant against the cognitive prison of egocentrism when listening to diverse voices.

The second law of thermodynamics states that the entropy of an isolated system never decreases—a stark fact that prompts me to reexamine the philosophical relationship between the arrow of time and the order inherent in life.

Van Gogh's 'The Starry Night' was created in 1889 during his stay at the Saint-Paul-de-Mausole asylum in Saint-Rémy, France, employing vigorous, swirling brushstrokes to convey subjective emotion and cosmic rhythm.
